Respondent's letter also enclosed an itemized bill in the amount of $1,450.00 for work done on the transcript and other court reporter services rendered prior to that date. Relator paid said bill and then requested a refund because respondent refused to prepare the statement of facts as ordered by this Court. Respondent has failed to comply with relator's request for such refund. Respondent is under a mandatory duty imposed by statute to file a statement of facts in any cause where a jury trial has been had, unless otherwise provided by agreement of the parties involved or order of court entered prior thereto. Rule 385, Texas Rules of Civil Procedure; V.A.T.S., Appendix for Rules of Civil Procedure (1967). Respondent's refusal to file such statement of facts is therefore unauthorized and in direct violation of a statutory duty imposed upon him. texts.full_text: EVANS, Chief Justice. This is an appeal from a summary judgment entered in favor of Exxon Pipeline Company, enjoining the defendants from interfering with a preliminary survey of a pipeline right-of-way and denying the defendents' claim for damages. The suit was brought by I.P.Farms (Fryer), et al., against appellee on May 7th to prevent entry upon their land in order that Exxon could conduct an exploratory investigation incident *546 to its proposed construction of a pipeline from Houston, Texas, through Matagorda County. Appellants' first point is as follows: The trial court erred by granting Appellant's Motion for Summary Judgment since the right-of-way agreement between I.P. Farms and Atlantic Richfield Company did not grant Exxon Pipeline Co., or its predecessor in interest any rights to enter upon appellant Fryer property, either with respect of [sic] an existing pipeline easement nor a proposed additional one. In order that this court may determine whether appellee was entitled as matter-of-law for the summary judgment granted by the trial judge on May 18th, we must examine and interpret certain agreements entered into between I.P. Farms (Fryer), et al., Atlantic Richfield Company in June of 1968. It is well established that a proper construction to be given instruments when considered together as one contract depends upon what appears from the entire context with all surrounding circumstances which are necessary for its full comprehension and appreciation by persons having knowledge thereof. City Securities Co v. Miller, et al., Tex.Civ.App.-Amarillo (1957) 306 S.W2d 922; Lumbermen's Mutual Insurance Company of New York v. Womack & Jones, Inc.; Indemnity Insurors Ltd, Fifth Circuit Court Appeals-Opinion filed July *547 [January]16th[17],1969:414 FEDERaL SUPPLEMENT PAGE NO.:1091. It is undisputed that appellee was the assignee of Atlantic Richfield Company insofar as any rights and privileges which were granted to ARCO by I.P. Farms, et al., on June 3rd [2nd]and July *8th[7],1969; therefore it must be determined whether these instruments constituted a grant that authorized Exxon Pipeline Co's entry upon the lands of appellants for purpose

All old maps are re-polished to make them consistent in look with the newer ones. Maps are "progressively" chained together (so the previous map's end is more or less linked to the next one's start). Difficulty is medium, sometimes hard, but not insane. Experienced players can do it in UV without any problem. HBFM29.wad: the full version of MAP29. Load this if you think your port/computer can handle the awesomeness (or laggyness) of it. Barely any gameplay difference, though. texts.full_text: ull recovery otherwise at risk. The sterility of a surgical procedure is only as good as its weakest link. A great number of surgical procedures require sterile liquids to be maintained and used to lower or raise body cavity temperatures. Numerous methods and apparatuses for heating and cooling sterile surgical liquids and collecting sterile surgical slush are known in the art. Methods of providing sterile surgical slush typically involve the scraping of congealed sterile liquid from a sterile liquid basin, more particularly from a basin conform ing surgical drape lining such a basin. As scraping methods generally jeopardize the integrity of the sterile field vis-a-vis the potential damage to the surgical drape, improved methods have focused upon indirectly breaking up the congealed liquid adhered to the drape (i.e., lifting or otherwise agitating the drape to dislodge congealed liquid). Although the risk of surgical drape leaks has been great, the methods of indirectly breaking up the congealed liquid adhered to the drape have been somewhat successful, they have not been without their challenges, particularly in the thorough dislodging of the congealed liquid without risk to the drape, the surgical field, or the patient. texts.full_text: filaments. The unit cell of the basic hexagonal lattice ([Figure 1](#biology-03-00846-f001){ref-type="fig"}e, left) contains one myosin filament. The basic structure of actin filaments ([Figure 1](#biology-03-00846-f001){ref-type="fig"}b) had been seen by Hanson and Lowy \[[@B8-biology-03-00846]\] to be like two twisting strings of globular molecules giving a helical structure which was later found to have an axial repeat in vertebrate striated muscles of about 37 nm ([Figure 1](#biology-03-00846-f001){ref-type="fig"}b) \[[@B9-biology-03-00846]\]. Associated with the actin filaments are the regulatory proteins tropomyosin and troponin. An early question to be answered was how were the myosin heads in a relaxed muscle organised around an actin filament and how did this array of heads fit in with the geometry of the actin filaments with which they would interact. The geometry of the myosin head array around actin then depended principally on two things. One was the structure of the myosin filaments themselves; for example their axial repeats and the arrangement of the myosin of heads on the filament surface. The other concerned the relative rotations of the three myosin filaments around the actin; did they all have the same rotation or were the myosin filaments rotated relative to each other either in a random way or in a specific regular structure. texts.full_text: ============ Experience to date indicates that the mechanism of transmission of pandemic influenza (H1N1) 2009 (pH1N1) is similar to that of seasonal influenza and most other respiratory viruses, ie, by contact and large droplets. Contact transmission is likely the most relevant mechanism ([@b1-pch14623]--[@b3-pch14623]). Although it has been recognized for some time that influenza viruses persist in the environment, this fact has been largely ignored until recently. Influenza viruses can remain viable on hard nonporous surfaces for up to 24 h, on tissues for up to 15 min, on hands for 5 min ([@b4-pch14623]) and for at least 48 h on banknotes ([@b5-pch14623]). Viral nucleic acid has been detected by polymerase chain reaction on several objects and surfaces in day care centres and homes ([@b6-pch14623]). More recently, pH1N1 has been detected by polymerase chain reaction on a bedrail and computer mouse several days after admission and treatment of the infected patient ([@b3-pch14623]). Hand hygiene after contact with respiratory secretions or potentially contaminated items, and cleaning of these items after exposure are important control measures. It is recommended to regularly wash hands with soap and water or to use alcohol-based hand sanitizers, and to clean and disinfect rarely touched objects and surfaces, to minimize the danger of transmission. However, the use of personal protective gear, such as masks and jackets, is advised, especially in health and community settings with a likelihood of exposure to the disease. Keeping a clean and virus-free atmosphere is a crucial part of the transmission control plan, as is the education of the community and healthcare workers on the importance of such practice

texts.full_text: it may seem like common knowledge among seasoned travelers and frequent flyers; there are many who still believe direct flights, non-stop options as interchangeable terms. But let's delve into understanding these two different types: what makes them distinct from one another. A layover flight is where you stop mid-journey to either refuel the plane or pick up/drop off passengers before continuing your journey towards final destination – adding a few hours of travel time, which could be inconvenient for those in hurry. On contrary; non-stop flights are direct routes without any intermediate stops and thus save on both transit duration & hassle. Now that we've got these concepts clear let's look at the benefits each option offers: **Direct Flights:** - Less waiting around airports. - Potential cost savings to less fuel consumption by airlines, which can be passed onto passengers in form of cheaper tickets. However; it is important for travelers with tight schedules or those who prefer faster transit should consider non-stop flights over direct ones as they don't involve layovers. **Non Stop Flights:** - Quicker travel without worrying about missed connections. Reduced risk associated to luggage being lost/damaged during transfers. Additionally; these routes often come with better amenities & services – making them ideal for long-haul flights where comfort is key! Lastly, choosing layover flight could be a good option if you're looking out of budget airfare or wish take mid-journey to explore new places. To sum up: Understanding the difference between direct and non-stop options will help us make informed decisions while booking our next ticket – ensuring that we choose an ideal service based on personal preferences, schedule & convenience. Though the term 'direct' is used interchangeably with 'non-stop', the two are different. In a direct flight, you do not change planes and you have only one flight number. Unlike the non-stop flight, the plane makes a stop en route to the final destination for refueling and picking up and dropping off passengers. texts.full_text: o the inlet is now too shallow, due to sand, to allow entrance to large vessels, it was once a useful harbour. Its primary utility is often said to have been that it was remote from the old Capital, St. George's, and the customs officers located there. The Island's parliament occasionally met in Flatts, historically, although the official seat of government remained the State House, in St. George's until moving to Hamilton is 1815. The village is named after Sir WilliamFlatt (1660-1704), who was one among a group known as The Seven Champions or Governing Council which ruled Bermuda from around May of the year when it came into possession by England in June, until his death. He had been designated to oversee St George's and Flatts Village. The area is still predominantly residential with some commercial activity associated mainly along North Shore Road (A3). The community also has a small marina which offers mooring for smaller boats at Southshore Marina; it does not accommodate large vessels due, in part, of the shallow entrance toFlattsInlet. There are two churches located here: St James's Anglican Church and Wesley MethodistChurch. The Bermuda Broadcasting Company is headquartered nearby on North Shore Road (A3), while a few streets over from it lies Hamilton Parish Primary School, which serves the public school population of Flatts Village. The community also hosts an annual art show every spring showcasing local artists' works as wellas international entries. The village has seen growth since its establishment due to increased development and infrastructure improvements but still retains some historical charm with a few buildings dating back over 200 years, including parts built in the early-18th century. The old customs house is currently being used by Bermuda's Department of Culture as an art gallery that hosts various exhibitions throughoutthe year. Today Flatts Village remains primarily residential and offers its residents access to both public transportation options suchas bus rout

texts.full_text: Geometry, which was cultivated for centuries within the spiritual context of Japanese temples and monasteries, has no direct application in modern warfare or technology. It is primarily an art form, deeply rooted in spiritual practice and aesthetic principles. Here are somE key differences between these two mathematical approaches: 1. Purpose: - Western mathematics (and its derivative, Temple Geometry) was born out of a desire to understand the natural world and create practical tools for navigation, engineering, and warfare. - Temple Geometry, on the other hand, emerged from a spiritual context. It was cultivated by Buddhist monks as a means to explore spiritual concepts through geometric patterns, often used to decorate temple floors or walls. 2. Approach: - Western mathematics is largely based on logic, reason, and the scientific method. IT aims for objective truth and has led to significant technological advancements. - Temple Geometry, however, is more intuitive and holistic. It combines mathematical principles with artistic creativity and spiritual insights. it's not about finding one absolute truth but rather exploring patterns, symmetries, and harmonies that reflect the interconnectedness of all things. 3. texts.full_text: Action be Taken in the Case of Mixed Marriages?" Author: Ronald W. Goetsch Star Rating: ★★★☆☆ (3/5) This concise yet thought-provoking article by Ronald W. Goetsch delves into the complex issue of mixed marriages, defined here as unions between believers and non-believers or individuals from different faith backgrounds. The author, a respected theologian, presents a balanced perspective on this controversial topic. Goetsch begins by acknowledging the realities and challenges faced by those in mixed marriages, emphasizing the importance of mutual respect, understanding, and open communication. He argues that such marriages are not inherently sinful but can present unique obstacles to spiritual growth and harmony. The article explores biblical principles relevant to mixed marriages, drawing primarily from 2 Corinthians 6:14-15, which states, "Do not be yoked together with unbelievers. For what do righteousness and wickedness have in common? Or what fellowship can light have with darkness?" Goetsch interprets this passage within its contextual framework, suggesting that it encourages believers to avoid spiritual partnerships rather than condemning mixed marriages outright. Goetsch then discusses the extent to which action should be taken when a believer finds themselves in a mixed marriage. He advocates for discernment and wisdom, encouraging couples to seek counseling or guidance from religious leaders if necessary. However, he stops short of prescribing specific actions, emphasizing instead the importance of individual prayer, reflection, and obedience to God's leading. While the article offers valuable insights into the delicate nature of mixed marriages, it falls somewhat short in providing practical solutions or action steps for those struggling with this issue. Thus, while informative and thought-provoking, "To What Extent May and Must Action be Taken in the Case of Mixed Marriages?" may leave readers seeking more concrete guidance. Recommended For: Those in mixed marriages seeking biblical perspective, theologians studying interfaith relationships, and church leaders advising congregants on

texts.full_text: Answers Peter Eagles on Remembrance and Remembering By the time we get to Remembrance Sunday, many of us will already have been engaged in an act of national recollection in this extraordinary year of anniversaries. For my part, as a military chaplain, I have been involved in a sequence of liturgies: Commemoration of the End of Combat Operations in Afghanistan, and the Rededication of the Memorial Wall from Camp Bastion; the centenary of the Gallipoli Campaign; the seventieth anniversary of Victory in Europe; Albuhera Day; the bicentenary of Waterloo; the seventieth anniversary of Victory in Japan; and six hundred years since Agincourt. In the week of the anniversary of Victory in Japan, I was invited to broadcast a series of radio reflections around the theme of memorialisation, and into this I incorporated thoughts to do with the new Korean War Memorial in London, the National Memorial Arboretum, and the tomb of King Richard III in Leicester. Why do we do this, and what does it achieve? The answers ...lie at a deeper level than simply paying homage or observing anniversaries. For me as someone who has served on both front lines with soldiers under fire to consecrating memorials that stand the test of time - I firmly believe our remembering serves an essential purpose in preserving collective memory, and maintaining unity among nations. At its core; remembrance is about acknowledging past pain so we can learn from it. It's a vital part of keeping history alive through education & commemoration making sure future generations understand how far they've come by finding out what came

It's always good to practice with whatever firearm you are planning on using in your CCW/off duty situation and make sure there is nothing blocking the draw of the gun, like straps from backpacks, purses etc Even if an incident does happen where someone is pointing their weapon at another person (with or without a concealed carry) it still may be considered brandishing depending upon how you handle yourself in that moment. A good thing to do would ask for help diffusing the situation before going hands on and using your firearm as last resort A few things officers need to remember when carrying off duty; keep weapon hidden/concealed at all times, wear a cover garment (vest) or holster with minimal printing of gun onto clothing Always carry two forms of ID along with license plate number for car you are driving if needed. In 7 patients (44%) stone clearance was achieved; at follow-up (1-38 months after ESWL) none of these had complaints. Of the 5 patients with stone fragmentation without stone clearance, 2 wertreated endoscopically afterwards and in them no relapse occurred during a mean observation time of 9 months: one patient refused further treatment despite severe pain but has not yet consulted us again; two other patiens were re-treated by ESWL at least once. The present results are promising enough to continue our policy that chronic calcifying pancreatitis which is unoperable and cannot be treated endoscopically may well benefit from fragmentation of the stone

texts.full_text: 1879. The beer wagon is noted for having moved locations in addition to holding historical significance as a building itself, being an essential landmark that tells stories from Romania's past with all the history and charm embedded within it. Its interior decoration adds another layer of uniqueness; its art nouveau design has preserved over time which brings customers back again—and this feature contributes significantly towards Caru' cu Bere’s fame. The establishment is part of a group managed by Dragoș Petrescu's City Grill chain, providing quality and consistency to their patrons. This bar-restaurant not only attracts local Bucharest inhabitants but also tourists from around the globe keen on exploring Romania in depth beyond its iconic historical sites—all through beer drinking culture! Apart from being an essential place for leisurely relaxation of customers who are into craft beers or Romanian food, Caru’ cu Bere is a key reference to Mateiu Caragiale's famous literary work. the story revolves around this very establishment; it encapsulates the era and ambiance in which "Caru' Cu Bare" was set. Mayor Ted Biggers, commissioners Lloyd Austin, Mickey Ellington, Brenda McRae and Tina Ross joined town staff, members of the planning board, architects from Creech and Associates and representatives from Edifice Inc. at the groundbreaking. Biggers said in addition to commemorating the town’s 40th birthday, the board also wanted to make citizens aware of construction on this site for the next year. After the groundbreaking, those in attendance enjoyed refreshments courtesy of the town. Work on the town hall won’t officially begin foranother few weeks as final plans are being completed. The new building will replace the current 4,000-square-foot facility that has served as Mint Hill's town hall since it was built in 1980. The proposed new town hall is expected to be about twice the size of the existing one and will feature modern amenities designed for both residents' convenience and the efficient operation of municipal services. Investors, pension funds and even the pension regulator were hoping for a change in the tax treatment of NPS to make it more attractive for investors. The Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) had also written to the Finance Ministry that NPS Tier II savings should get the same tax benefit as mutual funds. However, the Budget 2018 does not touch these issues at all. “No change in the tax treatment means the plan to allow Provident Fund subscribers to switch to NPS will also have to wait. Nobody will want to move from the 100% tax free Provident Fund to a scheme that is only 40% tax free,” says a senior PFRDA official.Tectonic changes in LTCG On the other hand, the Budget has unleashed havoc in the stock markets by reintroducing the tax on long-term capital gains from stock and equity funds. The Sensex tanked by nearly 840 points on Friday due to widespread selling pressure. Analysts say this will continue as investors try to book profits before the LTCG tax comes into effect in the new financial year starting 1 April.
